name: GitHub

auth.notes: For organization management, use OAuth apps or GitHub Apps. GitHub Apps are preferred and use installation tokens. For SCIM provisioning on Enterprise Cloud, use a personal access token (classic) with scim:enterprise scope. Fine-grained PATs support organization-level member and team permissions.

api.rateLimits: Authenticated: 5,000 requests/hour (15,000 for Enterprise Cloud). Unauthenticated: 60 requests/hour. GitHub Apps: min 5,000/hour, scales with repos/users. Secondary limits: 100 concurrent requests, 900 points/minute, 80 content-generating requests/minute. Org invitations: 50/day (free) or 500/day (paid/established org).

scim.notes: SCIM is available only on GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Enterprise Managed Users (EMU). Requires personal access token (classic) with scim:enterprise scope. Supports provisioning users and groups. Rate limit: max 1,000 users/hour through SCIM integration.

webhooks.notes: Organization webhooks can subscribe to member/team events. Repository webhooks cannot receive org-level events. GitHub Apps receive webhooks natively based on their configured permissions and subscribed events.

sandbox.notes: GitHub Free plan supports unlimited public/private repos with up to 3 collaborators on private repos. Organizations are free to create. For SCIM testing, GitHub Enterprise Cloud offers a 30-day free trial. No dedicated sandbox environment but free orgs work well for dev/test.

needs_verification: Whether GitHub Apps can access all org member management endpoints or only OAuth apps
Exact SCIM rate limits beyond the 1,000 users/hour guideline
Whether fine-grained PATs can be used for SCIM (docs reference classic PATs)
